summary: wsh4-nmp postinstall beacons installer path and host metadata to hardcoded Discord webhook
details: package.json declares scripts.postinstall = 'node index.js'. On install, index.js unconditionally POSTs installer-identifying data — the absolute __filename path (which reveals the OS username and home-directory layout), Node version, process.platform, and process.arch — to a hardcoded Discord webhook (discord.com/api/webhooks/1523336123319849031/...) controlled by the author. The beacon fires automatically at `npm install` with no consent, no opt-out, and no documented purpose disclosed to the installer. Package naming ('wsh4-nmp') and internal defaults ('whs4_typo') together with a README note about notifying on typos indicate the package is positioned as a typosquat probe whose function is to inform the author whenever an installer mistypes a target package name and pulls this one in.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
